evidence.q1: Sections are numbered 1 through 15 sequentially; cross-reference in Section 3 references Section 7, and Section 9 references Section 4, both present and resolved.
evidence.q2: Defined term "Approved Regions" is defined in Section 2 but never used anywhere else in the document.
evidence.q3: No vague time/quantity phrasing found; specific durations like "540 days", "60 days' prior notice", "30 days' advance notice" are used, and "prompt written notice" in emergency context is standard commercial phrasing, not out-of-register.
evidence.q4: No archaic terms; the document uses modern legal English without words like "whereas", "heretofore", or "witnesseth".
evidence.q5: All sentences are grammatically complete and no internal contradictions within single sentences were found.
evidence.q6: Tone and vocabulary remain consistent throughout, with uniform use of modern, straightforward legal prose from start to end.
